result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would give every child—regardless of birthplace, wealth, or circumstance—a fair start: clean water, safe shelter, nutritious food, basic health care, and access to high-quality education that nurtures curiosity, empathy, and critical thinking.  

Most other global problems—poverty, disease, conflict, environmental degradation—are self-reinforcing cycles that begin in the earliest years of life. Break the cycle early and you don’t just improve individual lives; you release trillions of dollars’ worth of previously untapped human potential, creativity, and cooperation. In short, a fair start is the single most leveraged intervention I can imagine; everything else we hope for in the world becomes easier once that foundation is in place.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
